project_overview.project_name: Djibouti Skills Development for Employment Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve employability and employment outcomes of technical and vocational education and training graduates with a focus on women, persons with disabilities and refugees in priority economic sectors.
project_overview.total_financing: US$15.00 million (IDA Credit: US$10.00 million, IDA Grant: US$5.00 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 6,000 students in diploma/certificate programs, 10,375 in short-term training, 5,400 in work-based learning, 2,250 in skills certification, with specific focus on women, persons with disabilities, and refugees

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - project specifically targets refugee populations concentrated in Ali Sabieh (Ali-Addeh and Holl-Holl camps), Obock, and Djibouti City

key_components.description: Work-based learning programs, online skills acquisition, labor mobility programs including internships, apprenticeships, OJT, and bilateral labor agreements

key_components.component_name: Improving TVET Sectoral & Institutional Governance, Architecture & Service Delivery Mechanisms
key_components.description: Modernizing education governance, Quality Improvement Packages, capacity building, monitoring and evaluation

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of National Education and Vocational Training (MENFOP)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee under Economic Development Council (CDE), Project Technical Committee with multi-ministry representation, Project Implementation Unit within DGETFP
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF, moderate risk rating with capacity building support
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Multi-level data gathering system, administrative data, periodic surveys, tracer studies, independent academic evaluation

identified_risks.risk_description: Political and governance challenges affecting project implementation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: High-level steering committee under CDE, multi-ministry coordination mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability, first engagement in multi-sectoral skills development
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Capacity building programs, embedding staff in existing structures, simplified project design

identified_risks.risk_description: Financial management and procurement capacity constraints, work overload from multiple projects
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Additional dedicated staff, continuous training, strengthened FM systems, commercial software acquisition

identified_risks.risk_description: Economic uncertainties due to COVID-19 pandemic impacts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Building system resilience, regular engagement with private sector stakeholders
Regular dialogue with UNHCR and partners, monitoring of protection framework

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks
ESS4: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level GRM with confidential reporting, specific GBV/SEA/SH mechanism
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: 50% target for women beneficiaries, addressing barriers including FGM/C (93% prevalence), financial barriers, discrimination, Code of Conduct for institutions

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed to support refugee integration into national systems while benefiting host communities, addressing potential tensions through inclusive programming
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Djibouti serves as stable haven in volatile Horn of Africa region, hosting 34,810 refugees and asylum seekers (3.4% of population) from neighboring conflict-affected countries
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Project includes security risk mitigation through institutional protocols, safe learning environments, and coordination with security agencies
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - 3,165 refugees directly supported, 23,420 total refugee population, concentrated in Ali-Addeh, Holl-Holl camps (Ali Sabieh region), Obock, and Djibouti City, with 57% in working age group
